palette_hint: Background #0f1117 (near-black), body text #c9d1d9 (light gray), primary accent #58a6ff (blue), secondary accent #3fb950 (green) for success states and code highlights.
type_style: Headings in system-ui bold (700), body in 'Courier New' monospace at 14px for code blocks and 15px Georgia serif for prose; clinical, technical feel with tight line-height of 1.5.
description: FerroLink is a fictional industrial IoT data-pipeline API whose documentation site features a fixed left sidebar for navigation, a scrollable main content area with multi-column reference tables, sticky section headers, and dense data UI layouts throughout.
constraints: Fixed left sidebar exactly 260px wide, background #161b22, with a scrollable nav tree of sections and subsections indented 16px each level; sidebar remains visible while main content scrolls.
Main content area has max-width 860px, left margin 260px, padding 40px 48px, so on wide screens content stays centered within the available right zone.

API reference page uses a two-column layout: a 60% left column for endpoint description and prose, and a 40% right column (background #0d1117) showing code samples in a sticky panel that follows scroll.

Data-models page uses a three-column table layout with columns 'Field', 'Type', and 'Description'; tables have 1px solid #30363d borders, alternating row backgrounds #161b22 and #0f1117, and a sticky thead row with background #1c2128.
All inline code spans use background #1c2128, color #f78166, border-radius 4px, padding 2px 6px; code blocks use background #161b22, border-left 3px solid #58a6ff, padding 16px, font-size 13px.

Sticky section headings (h2) have position sticky, top 0, background #0f1117, border-bottom 1px solid #30363d, padding 12px 0, z-index 10, to remain visible while scrolling through long reference sections.

page_specs.getting-started: Introduces FerroLink with a brief overview of its purpose—streaming sensor telemetry from industrial equipment into cloud pipelines—followed by a step-by-step quickstart: obtaining an API key, installing the ferrolink-sdk via npm, and making a first authenticated GET /streams request. Includes two code blocks (bash install command and a JS fetch example) and a two-column prerequisites table listing 'Requirement' and 'Minimum Version' for Node.js, OpenSSL, and the FerroLink gateway firmware.
page_specs.api-reference: Lists all 14 FerroLink REST endpoints grouped into three sticky-headed sections: Streams, Devices, and Alerts. Each endpoint row in the two-column layout shows the HTTP method badge (color-coded: GET=#3fb950, POST=#58a6ff, DELETE=#f78166), path, and prose description on the left, with a sticky right panel showing the curl example, request headers, and a sample JSON response body for whichever endpoint is in view.
page_specs.data-models: Documents six core JSON schemas—StreamObject, DeviceRecord, AlertPayload, TelemetryPoint, AuthToken, and WebhookEvent—each rendered as a dense three-column table (Field / Type / Description) with nested sub-rows for object properties. A sticky left mini-nav within the main column links to each model's anchor, and required fields are marked with a #f78166 asterisk in the Field column.
page_specs.changelog: Presents the release history of the FerroLink API from v1.0.0 through v3.4.1 in reverse chronological order, with each release as a card-like block (border 1px solid #30363d, padding 20px, margin-bottom 24px). Each block has a sticky version badge header, a categorized list of changes using colored labels ADDED (#3fb950), CHANGED (#58a6ff), DEPRECATED (#e3b341), and FIXED (#f78166), and a two-column diff table for breaking changes showing 'Before' and 'After' values.
page_specs.error-codes: Provides a filterable reference table of all 47 FerroLink HTTP and application-level error codes, with columns Code, HTTP Status, Short Name, and Resolution. Rows are grouped under sticky category headers: Authentication (4xx), Rate Limiting, Stream Errors, Device Errors, and Internal (5xx). Each Short Name is rendered as an inline code span, and the Resolution column contains concise fix instructions referencing specific API parameters or dashboard settings.
